subject: Physics
summary: Thermodynamics is a branch of physics that deals with the relationships between heat, work, and energy. It is based on four laws: zeroth law, first law, second law, and third law. The zeroth law states that if two systems are in thermal equilibrium with a third system, then they are also in thermal equilibrium with each other. The first law defines internal energy as the sum of kinetic and potential energies. The second law introduces entropy, which is a measure of disorder or randomness. The third law specifies how entropy changes during reversible processes. Thermodynamics has many applications in engineering, chemistry, and biology. For JEE Mains and NEET UG students, understanding thermodynamics concepts such as ideal gas equation, internal energy, enthalpy, and free energy will help them solve problems efficiently. It is essential to practice solving thermodynamics-related questions for both exams.

questions.question: The zeroth law of thermodynamics states that if two systems are in thermal equilibrium with a third system, then they are also in thermal equilibrium with each other. What does this mean?

options.A: It means the temperature of all three systems is same
options.B: It means the heat transfer between the systems is same
options.C: It means the internal energy of all three systems is same
options.D: It means the entropy of all three systems is same

questions.question: What is the difference between internal energy and enthalpy?

options.A: Internal energy is a state function while enthalpy is not
options.B: Enthalpy is a state function while internal energy is not
options.C: Both are state functions
options.D: Neither is a state function

questions.question: Ideal gas equation states PV = nRT. What does P represent?

options.A: Pressure
options.B: Volume
options.C: Number of moles
options.D: Temperature

questions.question: The entropy change during a reversible isothermal process is zero. What is the reason?

options.A: Because there is no heat transfer
options.B: Because the temperature remains constant
options.C: Because the pressure remains constant
options.D: Because there is no work done

questions.question: What is the significance of free energy in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the spontaneity of a process
options.B: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.C: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.D: It determines the enthalpy change during a process

questions.question: What is the difference between adiabatic and isothermal processes?

options.A: Adiabatic process is reversible while isothermal process is irreversible
options.B: Isothermal process is reversible while adiabatic process is irreversible
options.C: Adiabatic process occurs at constant temperature while isothermal process occurs at constant pressure
options.D: Isothermal process occurs at constant temperature while adiabatic process occurs at constant volume

questions.question: What is the significance of entropy in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the spontaneity of a process
options.B: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.C: It determines the enthalpy change during a process
options.D: It determines the disorder or randomness of a system

questions.question: What is the difference between heat and work?

options.A: Heat is energy transfer due to temperature difference while work is energy transfer due to pressure difference
options.B: Work is energy transfer due to temperature difference while heat is energy transfer due to pressure difference
options.C: Both are forms of internal energy
options.D: Neither is a form of internal energy

questions.question: What is the significance of Carnot cycle in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the efficiency of an engine
options.B: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.C: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.D: It determines the free energy change during a process

questions.question: What is the difference between reversible and irreversible processes?

options.A: Reversible process occurs at constant temperature while irreversible process occurs at constant pressure
options.B: Irreversible process occurs at constant temperature while reversible process occurs at constant pressure
options.C: Reversible process is a spontaneous process while irreversible process is non-spontaneous
options.D: Irreversible process is a spontaneous process while reversible process is non-spontaneous

questions.question: What is the significance of Gibbs free energy in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the spontaneity of a process
options.B: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.C: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.D: It determines the enthalpy change during a process

questions.question: What is the difference between ideal and real gases?

options.A: Ideal gas has zero viscosity while real gas has non-zero viscosity
options.B: Real gas has zero viscosity while ideal gas has non-zero viscosity
options.C: Ideal gas obeys kinetic molecular theory while real gas does not
options.D: Real gas obeys kinetic molecular theory while ideal gas does not

questions.question: What is the significance of Kelvin-Planck statement in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the efficiency of an engine
options.B: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.C: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.D: It determines the free energy change during a process

questions.question: What is the difference between adiabatic and isothermal compressions?

options.A: Adiabatic compression occurs at constant temperature while isothermal compression occurs at constant pressure
options.B: Isothermal compression occurs at constant temperature while adiabatic compression occurs at constant pressure
options.C: Adiabatic compression occurs at constant volume while isothermal compression occurs at constant temperature
options.D: Isothermal compression occurs at constant volume while adiabatic compression occurs at constant temperature

questions.question: What is the significance of Joule-Thomson coefficient in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.B: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.C: It determines the enthalpy change during a process
options.D: It determines the isothermal compressibility of a gas

questions.question: What is the difference between ideal and non-ideal solutions?

options.A: Ideal solution has zero osmotic pressure while non-ideal solution has non-zero osmotic pressure
options.B: Non-ideal solution has zero osmotic pressure while ideal solution has non-zero osmotic pressure
options.C: Ideal solution obeys Raoult's law while non-ideal solution does not
options.D: Non-ideal solution obeys Raoult's law while ideal solution does not

questions.question: What is the significance of van't Hoff equation in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.B: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.C: It determines the enthalpy change during a process
options.D: It relates the chemical potential of a solute to its concentration

questions.question: What is the difference between ideal and real semiconductors?

options.A: Ideal semiconductor has zero carrier density while real semiconductor has non-zero carrier density
options.B: Real semiconductor has zero carrier density while ideal semiconductor has non-zero carrier density
options.C: Ideal semiconductor obeys band theory while real semiconductor does not
options.D: Real semiconductor obeys band theory while ideal semiconductor does not

questions.question: What is the significance of Nernst equation in thermodynamics?

options.A: It determines the entropy change during a process
options.B: It determines the internal energy change during a process
options.C: It determines the enthalpy change during a process
options.D: It relates the electrochemical potential of an ion to its concentration

questions.question: What is the difference between ideal and real superconductors?

options.A: Ideal superconductor has zero resistivity while real superconductor has non-zero resistivity
options.B: Real superconductor has zero resistivity while ideal superconductor has non-zero resistivity
options.C: Ideal superconductor obeys BCS theory while real superconductor does not
options.D: Real superconductor obeys BCS theory while ideal superconductor does not
